skill.name: turborepo
skill.description: Guidance for configuring Turborepo monorepos, task pipelines, caching, filtering, CI workflows, environment variables, and package structure.
skill.summary: Configure and optimize Turborepo monorepos with focused guidance for tasks, caching, CI, filters, and package boundaries.
skill.author: antfu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-risk matches, but review found them in Markdown documentation and configuration examples rather than executable code. No malicious intent or prompt injection was found, but the skill can guide CI, remote cache, command, filesystem, and environment-variable changes, so publication should include an operational-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Operational Build and CI Command Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill contains many Turborepo, package-manager, and CI command examples. These are legitimate documentation examples, but users may execute them in local repositories or CI systems.
medium_findings.reasoning: The scanner label for Ruby backtick execution is a false match, but the underlying command guidance is real. Commands are ordinary Turborepo and CI commands, not obfuscated or malicious.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are command examples in Markdown and workflow snippets. They can affect build behavior if copied, but I found no executable wrapper or hidden command runner.

medium_findings.title: Environment and Secret Configuration Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill discusses CI tokens, API keys, database URLs, and pass-through environment variables. It uses placeholder names, but it can influence secret exposure and cache behavior.
medium_findings.reasoning: The references explicitly teach how to pass tokens and environment variables to builds. This is appropriate for Turborepo, but users should avoid exposing actual secret values in prompts or generated files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines clearly name CI tokens, database URLs, and API keys. I did not find evidence of hardcoded real credentials or exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Remote Cache and External Service Configuration
medium_findings.description: The skill includes guidance for Vercel remote cache, self-hosted cache endpoints, and GitHub Actions tokens. These network references are expected for Turborepo workflows.
medium_findings.reasoning: The skill intentionally documents remote cache services and external endpoints. I found no automatic network request or unauthorized data transfer in the skill files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is documented configuration rather than code. Risk depends on how users configure tokens and cache endpoints.

low_findings.title: Static Command-Execution Matches Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Most external command findings are backticked Markdown snippets or fenced examples. They are not Ruby shell execution and are not executed by the skill itself.
low_findings.reasoning: The scanner treated Markdown backticks as Ruby backtick shell execution. The files are documentation and command examples, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are Markdown and the surrounding context is explanatory. No Ruby source file or dynamic command runner was found.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Strings Are Anti-Pattern Examples
low_findings.description: Path traversal sequences appear in examples that explain what not to do, or how to reference root files correctly in Turborepo.
low_findings.reasoning: The suspicious paths are illustrative configuration snippets. They do not read or write arbitrary files from the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced sections label traversal patterns as wrong or discuss monorepo task examples. No file-access implementation is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Cache Hash Terminology
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography findings are caused by common Turborepo terms such as hash, cache key, and signature. The skill does not implement cryptography.
low_findings.reasoning: The cited content explains build-cache hashing and remote-cache signature settings. There is no MD5, SHA1, or custom cryptographic implementation in executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are documentation for Turborepo cache mechanics. The scanner appears to have matched security-sensitive words without executable context.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance and C2 Keyword Alerts Lack Malicious Context
low_findings.description: System, network, and C2 keyword matches are ordinary documentation terms, command names, or a Git SHA in sync metadata.
low_findings.reasoning: The static matches do not form command-and-control behavior, host enumeration, or hidden execution. They are normal words and metadata in a documentation skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: I found no encoded payload, external callback, or instruction to collect system data. The SYNC.md finding is only source metadata.

dangerous_patterns.title: No Confirmed Malicious Dangerous Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer reported a dangerous combination of code execution, network, filesystem, and credential access. Manual review found documentation examples for these topics, not executable malicious logic.
dangerous_patterns.reasoning: The risky categories co-occur because the skill teaches Turborepo build configuration. No prompt injection, exfiltration instruction, hidden script, or obfuscated payload was found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Representative high-risk locations are plain Markdown guidance. The package file list contains Markdown and metadata only, with no executable helper script.

content.value_statement: Large monorepos often waste time on slow builds, unclear task pipelines, and cache misses. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code configure Turborepo workflows with practical patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to structure Turborepo workspaces with apps and packages.
Guides task configuration for dependsOn, outputs, inputs, env, cache, and persistent tasks.
Provides patterns for remote caching with Vercel or self-hosted cache services.
Shows how to filter packages with --affected, --filter, package names, directories, and Git refs.
Helps design GitHub Actions and Vercel workflows for Turborepo projects.
Documents common cache, environment, and package-boundary mistakes.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a dedicated Turborepo parser or validator.
It does not verify live Turborepo behavior without running commands in the user repository.
It may need project-specific context before changing package scripts or CI files.
It should not receive real secret values in prompts or generated examples.

use_cases.title: Create a New Monorepo Pipeline
use_cases.description: Set up package-level build, lint, test, and typecheck tasks with cacheable outputs and clear root delegation.

use_cases.title: Fix Cache Misses in CI
use_cases.description: Review Turborepo inputs, environment variables, outputs, and remote cache settings that affect repeat build performance.

use_cases.title: Run Only Changed Packages
use_cases.description: Use --affected and --filter patterns to test the packages impacted by a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Turbo Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Turborepo setup. Check package scripts, turbo.json tasks, outputs, and common cache mistakes. Explain the changes before editing files.

prompt_templates.title: Add Package Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Turborepo tasks for build, lint, test, and typecheck. Prefer package tasks, keep the root package.json as delegation only, and update turbo.json.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize CI Builds
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve pull request and main branch workflows
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize our CI workflow for Turborepo. Use affected package execution where appropriate, configure cache behavior, and protect secrets.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Remote Cache Misses
prompt_templates.prompt: Investigate why Turborepo remote cache misses occur. Compare task inputs, environment variables, outputs, package dependencies, and remote cache settings.

output_examples.input: Review a monorepo where builds run sequentially from the root package.
output: Identifies root scripts that contain task logic.
Recommends moving build and test commands into each package.
Explains how package tasks improve parallel execution and cache reuse.

output_examples.input: Diagnose why a build ignores changes in environment files.
output: Checks whether relevant environment files are included in task inputs.
Separates variables that affect outputs from runtime-only credentials.
Warns against sharing actual secret values during debugging.

output_examples.input: Improve pull request checks for a large workspace.
output: Suggests changed-package execution for build, test, and lint tasks.
Includes dependent packages when shared packages change.
Notes when full runs are still needed for release or main branch validation.

best_practices: Use package-level tasks and keep root scripts as Turborepo delegation commands.
Declare outputs, inputs, and environment variables so cache keys match real build behavior.
Use strict environment handling and store tokens in CI secret managers.
anti_patterns: Putting all build logic in the root package.json.
Using broad environment file globs without understanding cache impact.
Passing real tokens, database URLs, or API keys into prompts or examples.

faq.question: Does this skill run Turborepo commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Command execution depends on the host tool and user approval settings.

faq.question: Can it create a turbo.json file?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help draft or edit task configuration when project files are available.

faq.question: Can it fix remote cache problems?
faq.answer: It can guide diagnosis of tokens, team settings, outputs, inputs, and environment variables that affect remote cache behavior.

faq.question: Is it only for Vercel projects?
faq.answer: No. It covers Vercel remote cache, self-hosted cache options, GitHub Actions, and general Turborepo configuration.

faq.question: Should I share secret values with it?
faq.answer: No. Share variable names and configuration shape only. Keep actual tokens and database URLs in secret managers.

faq.question: Does it replace official Turborepo documentation?
faq.answer: No. It is a practical workflow guide and should be checked against current project requirements.
